message.content: please watch this video: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=HTtKD5ZkJfk&list=LL using the yt watch skill ( @claude-skills/watch-yt/ )  and document in a tracker revit-annotations.md whether all of them are implemented in our software or not. if any is missing, ensure that we implement them fully; for each missing / added insight, create a branch, implement it, push, and then merge with main

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
1	---
2	name: watch-yt
3	description: Use this skill whenever the user asks Claude to "watch a video", "look at this YouTube link", "describe what happens in this video", or hands over a YouTube URL and wants to know its content. The skill runs a tiny Python script that passes the URL to Gemini, which watches the full video and returns a granular timestamped log (one entry per scene/topic/action change). Use the output to answer the user's question — summarise, find a moment, or reason over the content.
4	---
5	
6	# Watch-YT — let Gemini watch a YouTube video for you
7	
8	You cannot watch videos yourself. This skill delegates that to Gemini via a one-shot Python script and hands you back a dense, timestamped transcript you can reason over.
9	
10	## When to use
11	
12	Trigger phrases:
13	- "watch this video": `https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=…`
14	- "what happens in this YT link"
15	- "describe the video at [URL]"
16	- "summarise this YouTube video"
17	- any message containing a YouTube URL where the user wants content information
18	
19	## How to run
20	
21	```bash
22	# Text log only (fast)
23	python3 claude-skills/watch-yt/watch_yt.py "<YOUTUBE_URL>"
24	
25	# Text log + frame screenshots extracted at every timestamp
26	python3 claude-skills/watch-yt/watch_yt.py "<YOUTUBE_URL>" --screenshots /tmp/my-video
27	```
28	
29	Run from the repo root (`/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai`). The script reads
30	`GEMINI_API_KEY` from `.env` automatically.
31	
32	Optional flags:
33	- `--model gemini-2.5-pro` — higher quality analysis on complex videos
34	- `--screenshots <dir>` — also download the video (via yt-dlp) and extract a
35	  PNG frame at every timestamp with ffmpeg; writes `log.txt` + numbered PNGs
36	
37	## What you get back
38	
39	**Text mode** — a plain-text timestamped log:
40	
41	```
42	[00:00] Intro screen — title card "Building a BIM Model from Scratch"
43	[00:12] Speaker introduces themselves, explains the agenda for the session
44	[01:05] Opens the bim-ai dev environment; shows the empty canvas
45	...
46	```
47	
48	**Screenshot mode** — the same log plus a directory of numbered PNGs:
49	
50	```
51	/tmp/my-video/
52	  001_00-00.png
53	  002_00-12.png
54	  003_01-05.png
55	  ...
56	  log.txt
57	```
58	
59	## What to do with it
60	
61	- **Summarise**: condense the log into a paragraph for the user.
62	- **Find a moment**: locate the timestamp where X happens and report it.
63	- **Answer a question**: reason over the log to answer content questions.
64	- **Use in downstream tasks**: if the video is a tutorial relevant to the
65	  current code task, extract relevant steps from the log and apply them.
66	
67	## Dependencies
68	
69	- `google-genai>=1.65.0` — already in the conda env on this machine.
70	- `python-dotenv` — for `.env` loading.
71	- `GEMINI_API_KEY` in `.env` — see `.env.example` for the variable name.
72	
73	## Limitations
74	
75	- Gemini can watch videos up to ~1 hour reliably; very long videos may be
76	  truncated.
77	- Private or age-restricted YouTube videos will fail with a permissions error.
78	- Timestamp precision is ±5 s on fast-paced content; verify exact frames
79	  manually if precision matters.
80

file.content:
---
name: watch-yt
description: Use this skill whenever the user asks Claude to "watch a video", "look at this YouTube link", "describe what happens in this video", or hands over a YouTube URL and wants to know its content. The skill runs a tiny Python script that passes the URL to Gemini, which watches the full video and returns a granular timestamped log (one entry per scene/topic/action change). Use the output to answer the user's question — summarise, find a moment, or reason over the content.
---

# Watch-YT — let Gemini watch a YouTube video for you

You cannot watch videos yourself. This skill delegates that to Gemini via a one-shot Python script and hands you back a dense, timestamped transcript you can reason over.

## When to use

Trigger phrases:
- "watch this video": `https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=…`
- "what happens in this YT link"
- "describe the video at [URL]"
- "summarise this YouTube video"
- any message containing a YouTube URL where the user wants content information

## How to run

```bash
# Text log only (fast)
python3 claude-skills/watch-yt/watch_yt.py "<YOUTUBE_URL>"

# Text log + frame screenshots extracted at every timestamp
python3 claude-skills/watch-yt/watch_yt.py "<YOUTUBE_URL>" --screenshots /tmp/my-video
```

Run from the repo root (`/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai`). The script reads
`GEMINI_API_KEY` from `.env` automatically.

Optional flags:
- `--model gemini-2.5-pro` — higher quality analysis on complex videos
- `--screenshots <dir>` — also download the video (via yt-dlp) and extract a
  PNG frame at every timestamp with ffmpeg; writes `log.txt` + numbered PNGs

## What you get back

**Text mode** — a plain-text timestamped log:

```
[00:00] Intro screen — title card "Building a BIM Model from Scratch"
[00:12] Speaker introduces themselves, explains the agenda for the session
[01:05] Opens the bim-ai dev environment; shows the empty canvas
...
```

**Screenshot mode** — the same log plus a directory of numbered PNGs:

```
/tmp/my-video/
  001_00-00.png
  002_00-12.png
  003_01-05.png
  ...
  log.txt
```

## What to do with it

- **Summarise**: condense the log into a paragraph for the user.
- **Find a moment**: locate the timestamp where X happens and report it.
- **Answer a question**: reason over the log to answer content questions.
- **Use in downstream tasks**: if the video is a tutorial relevant to the
  current code task, extract relevant steps from the log and apply them.

## Dependencies

- `google-genai>=1.65.0` — already in the conda env on this machine.
- `python-dotenv` — for `.env` loading.
- `GEMINI_API_KEY` in `.env` — see `.env.example` for the variable name.

## Limitations

- Gemini can watch videos up to ~1 hour reliably; very long videos may be
  truncated.
- Private or age-restricted YouTube videos will fail with a permissions error.
- Timestamp precision is ±5 s on fast-paced content; verify exact frames
  manually if precision matters.

content.text: Now I'll run the watch-yt script on the video.

input.description: Watch YouTube video about Revit annotations via Gemini

content.content:
[00:00:00] A video intro animation shows a golden skyline against a dark, dotted background.
[00:00:03] The speaker, Balkan Architect, begins his introduction, explaining that the video will cover every single tool on the Annotation tab in Revit.
[00:00:12] The speaker mentions he has created similar videos covering the Architecture and Structure tabs.
[00:00:33] The speaker explains that the video will cover every tool on the Annotate tab, including some that viewers may have never used.
[00:00:57] The speaker promotes his website, balkanarchitect.com, showing the homepage with various Revit courses.
[00:01:17] The speaker scrolls through his website, highlighting course content, customized Revit templates, and high-quality Revit families.
[00:01:28] The speaker asks viewers to like the video and subscribe to his channel, showing the YouTube like and subscribe buttons.
[00:01:37] An image of an alpaca appears on the left side of the screen as the speaker says it makes the alpaca happy.
[00:01:45] The speaker transitions to the Revit interface, showing a 3D model of a two-story house.
[00:01:55] The speaker switches to the Level 1 floor plan view to demonstrate the annotation tools.
[00:02:11] The speaker mentions that the project file used in the tutorial is available for download on his Patreon page. An image collage of his Patreon projects is shown.
[00:02:23] Back in Revit, the speaker navigates to the "Annotate" tab on the ribbon.
[00:02:27] The speaker lists the panels on the Annotate tab: Dimension, Detail, Text, Tag, Color Fill, and Symbol.
[00:02:37] The speaker starts with the Dimension panel, specifically the Aligned and Linear dimension tools.
[00:02:52] The speaker demonstrates the "Aligned Dimension" tool (shortcut: DI) by dimensioning a straight wall and then an angled wall.
[00:03:25] The speaker demonstrates the "Linear Dimension" tool, showing how it only measures the horizontal or vertical distance of the angled wall, unlike the Aligned Dimension.
[00:04:00] The speaker moves on to the "Angular Dimension" tool and demonstrates it by measuring the angle between two angled walls.
[00:04:16] The speaker demonstrates the "Radial Dimension" tool on a circular wall, showing the radius.
[00:04:43] The speaker demonstrates the "Diameter Dimension" tool on another circular wall, showing the diameter.
[00:04:53] The speaker demonstrates the "Arc Length" tool on a curved wall segment.
[00:05:32] The speaker explains the "Spot Elevation" tool (shortcut: EL) and places markers on the floor plan, a stair landing, and a ramp, showing the different elevation values.
[00:06:23] The speaker demonstrates the "Spot Coordinate" tool, placing a marker that shows the North and East coordinates relative to the project base point.
[00:07:00] The speaker demonstrates the "Spot Slope" tool on a ramp, placing a slope symbol.
[00:07:14] The speaker selects the spot slope symbol, goes into "Edit Type," and changes the "Units Format" from a ratio to "Percentage" to display the slope differently.
[00:07:42] The speaker moves on to the Detail panel, starting with the "Detail Line" tool (shortcut: DL).
[00:08:00] The speaker demonstrates drawing with Detail Lines, using various draw tools like line, arc, and changing the Line Style to "Wide Lines," "Lines" (which are green), and "Overhead."
[00:08:52] The speaker explains the "Region" tool, which includes Filled Region and Masking Region.
[00:08:57] The speaker demonstrates creating a "Filled Region," drawing a custom shape, and showing how the boundary lines can be individually changed to different line styles, including "Invisible Lines."
[00:10:35] The speaker demonstrates the "Masking Region" tool by drawing a circle over a stair to hide it, using "Invisible Lines" for the boundary.
[00:11:08] The speaker moves to the "Component" tool, which includes Detail Component and Repeating Detail Component.
[00:11:18] The speaker places a "Detail Component" (a US-Universal Beam Section) and demonstrates using the "Bring to Front" option to place it over a masking region.
[00:11:42] The speaker demonstrates the "Repeating Detail Component" by selecting "Brick" and drawing a line, which automatically creates a repeating brick detail.
[00:12:03] The speaker demonstrates the "Revision Cloud" tool by drawing a cloud shape.
[00:12:21] The speaker demonstrates the "Insulation" tool, drawing a line of insulation and adjusting its "Insulation Width" and "Insulation Bulge to Width Ratio" in the Properties panel.
[00:12:59] The speaker explains "Detail Groups" by selecting multiple detail items, clicking "Create Group," naming it "Group 1," and then placing another instance of that group.
[00:13:38] The speaker moves to the Text panel, starting with the "Text" tool.
[00:13:41] The speaker places a text box, types "Text," and shows how to change the text type in the properties panel to adjust its size (e.g., from 2.5mm to 7mm).
[00:14:07] The speaker demonstrates the "Check Spelling" tool, first on a correctly spelled word and then on a misspelled word, showing the spell check dialog window with suggestions.
[00:14:36] The speaker demonstrates the "Find/Replace" tool, finding the word "Text" and replacing it with "ABC."
[00:15:02] The speaker moves to the Tag panel.
[00:15:07] The speaker demonstrates the "Tag by Category" tool by tagging a wall and a door.
[00:15:36] The speaker demonstrates the "Tag All" tool, selecting "Door Tags" and automatically tagging all untagged doors in the view.
[00:15:59] The speaker explains the "Beam Annotations" tool, showing the dialog box for setting up complex annotations for structural beams.
[00:17:05] The speaker demonstrates the "Multi-Category Tag" tool, loading a tag and applying it to a window and a floor, showing that it reports the "Type Mark" parameter.
[00:18:12] The speaker demonstrates the "Material Tag" tool, tagging individual material layers within a wall assembly, such as "Common brick" and "Gypsum Wall Board."
[00:19:04] The speaker explains the Room, Space, and Area tags, demonstrating the "Room Tag" tool on existing rooms in the plan.
[00:19:28] The speaker demonstrates the "Tread Number" tool, which automatically places numbers on each step of the staircase.
[00:20:04] The speaker demonstrates the "Revision Cloud" tool again.
[00:20:11] The speaker explains that "Revisions" have more functionality and that he has a dedicated video on the topic.
[00:20:20] The speaker moves back to the Detail panel to cover "Detail Group" and "Insulation," which he missed earlier. He then returns to the Tag panel.
[00:21:03] The speaker explains the "Multi-Rebar Annotation" tool is for structural projects.
[00:21:21] The speaker moves to the "Keynote" tool, explaining the Keynoting Settings and demonstrating placing a "Material Keynote" by tagging a wall, which brings up the Keynotes dialog to select a specific note.
[00:22:00] The speaker moves to the "Color Fill" panel.
[00:22:04] The speaker demonstrates the "Color Fill Legend" by placing it in the view, choosing to color "Rooms" by "Name." The rooms are colored, and a legend is created.
[00:22:31] To demonstrate the legend updating, the speaker changes the name of "Room 1" to "Room1," which creates a new entry and color in the legend and updates the room's fill color.
[00:22:47] The speaker switches to a different Revit project showing a plumbing layout to demonstrate the "Pipe Legend" and "Duct Legend."
[00:23:05] The speaker places a "Pipe Legend" and chooses the "Pipe Color Fill - Size" scheme. This colors all pipes in the view according to their size and creates a corresponding legend.
[00:23:40] The speaker moves to the "Symbol" panel.
[00:23:41] The speaker demonstrates the "Symbol" tool, placing a "Centerline" symbol and a "North Arrow" symbol.
[00:24:25] The speaker demonstrates the "Stair Path" symbol, adding an "UP" arrow to the staircase.
[00:24:33] The speaker demonstrates the "Span Direction" symbol on the previously created floor slab.
[00:24:56] The speaker concludes the overview of all tools on the Annotate tab.
[00:25:33] The video ends with an outro screen showing suggested videos and a link to the Balkan Architect website.

lastPrompt: please watch this video: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=HTtKD5ZkJfk&list=LL using the yt watch skill ( @claude-skills/watch-yt/ )  and document in a tracker revit-annotations.md whether all of them a…

content.text: Good, I have the full video log. Now let me explore our codebase to understand what annotation features we currently have before creating the tracker.

input.description: Explore annotation features in bim-ai codebase
input.prompt:
Search the bim-ai codebase at /Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai for all annotation-related features. I need to understand what annotation tools are implemented.

The Revit Annotate tab has these categories of tools:
- Dimensions: Aligned, Linear, Angular, Radial, Diameter, Arc Length, Spot Elevation, Spot Coordinate, Spot Slope
- Detail: Detail Line, Filled Region, Masking Region, Detail Component, Repeating Detail Component, Revision Cloud, Insulation, Detail Groups
- Text: Text, Check Spelling, Find/Replace
- Tags: Tag by Category, Tag All, Beam Annotations, Multi-Category Tag, Material Tag, Room Tag, Tread Number, Multi-Rebar Annotation, Keynote
- Color Fill: Color Fill Legend, Pipe Legend, Duct Legend
- Symbols: Symbol, Stair Path, Span Direction

Search for:
1. Any files mentioning "annotation", "dimension", "tag", "detail line", "filled region", "masking region", "keynote", "color fill", "spot elevation", "spot coordinate", "stair path", "span direction"
2. Any command files, model classes, or UI components related to these concepts
3. Look in app/bim_ai/, packages/web/src/, packages/cli/

Give me a thorough report of which annotation features exist in the codebase, with file paths. Search breadth: very thorough.

content.text:
Perfect! Now let me compile the final comprehensive report:

## Annotation Features in BIM-AI Codebase - Comprehensive Report

I've completed a thorough search of the bim-ai codebase for annotation-related features. Here's what I found:

### **IMPLEMENTED ANNOTATION FEATURES**

#### **1. Dimensions**
- **Element**: `DimensionElem`
- **Command**: `CreateDimensionCmd`
- **Files**:

- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/engine_dispatch_core.py` - Handles level-based dimensions and grid lines

This comprehensive implementation covers the core annotation workflow (dimensions, tags, detail geometry, and text) with auto-generation capabilities, but lacks the specialized dimension types, spot annotations, and advanced annotation features that Revit provides.

toolUseResult.prompt:
Search the bim-ai codebase at /Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai for all annotation-related features. I need to understand what annotation tools are implemented.

The Revit Annotate tab has these categories of tools:
- Dimensions: Aligned, Linear, Angular, Radial, Diameter, Arc Length, Spot Elevation, Spot Coordinate, Spot Slope
- Detail: Detail Line, Filled Region, Masking Region, Detail Component, Repeating Detail Component, Revision Cloud, Insulation, Detail Groups
- Text: Text, Check Spelling, Find/Replace
- Tags: Tag by Category, Tag All, Beam Annotations, Multi-Category Tag, Material Tag, Room Tag, Tread Number, Multi-Rebar Annotation, Keynote
- Color Fill: Color Fill Legend, Pipe Legend, Duct Legend
- Symbols: Symbol, Stair Path, Span Direction

Search for:
1. Any files mentioning "annotation", "dimension", "tag", "detail line", "filled region", "masking region", "keynote", "color fill", "spot elevation", "spot coordinate", "stair path", "span direction"
2. Any command files, model classes, or UI components related to these concepts
3. Look in app/bim_ai/, packages/web/src/, packages/cli/

Give me a thorough report of which annotation features exist in the codebase, with file paths. Search breadth: very thorough.
